local_validation.summary: The old app.routers.utils fallback export surface is gone. Tests now import parity/fallback helpers from their owning kernel_* route-family modules. The readiness harness no longer carries copied CPython bodies for coherence_weight, nodeid_distance, nodeid_compatibility, and weighted_average; it imports the canonical route-family fallback functions instead. Focused endpoint tests passed: 46 passed, 5 warnings. Harness smoke for nodeid_distance matched cpython=7 and kernel=7, with p50 CPython 0.0002ms and p50 subprocess kernel 5.2710ms.

evidence_refs: api/app/routers/utils.py now gathers route families and exposes kernel status only; it no longer re-exports fallback helpers.

The nine focused utility route tests now import fallback/parity helpers directly from app.routers.kernel_nodeid, app.routers.kernel_scoring, app.routers.kernel_grounding, or app.routers.kernel_grounded_cv.
scripts/kernel_readiness_harness.py imports canonical route-family fallback functions instead of carrying copied CPython implementations for the four benchmark cases.
rg stale-surface scan returned no matches for app.routers.utils fallback imports, utils.<fallback> references, or the removed copied readiness helper definitions.
api/app/routers/INDEX.md was regenerated from the new utils.py docstring.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: No endpoint behavior changes. Utility routes still register through /api/utils, but Python fallback helpers have one canonical route-family import surface instead of also being exported through app.routers.utils; readiness benchmarking now uses the same canonical helpers.
test_flows: Focused utility route tests passed with canonical fallback imports.
Readiness harness smoke matched CPython and kernel values for nodeid_distance.
Repository index and wellness readings stayed aligned.
